title: Quadlets running in LXC containers can leak netns
body:
### Issue Description

I am running rootful podman in an unprivileged LXC container. When using quadlets in lxc containers, old netns can be orphaned and leak when restarting the service while ssh'd into the container. /run/user/0 seems to just be a normal directory when the quadlet starts after the lxc container has been restarted, so /run/user/0/netns gets created as a tmpfs and netns is the only thing in /run/user/0. Then if you ssh into the container a new tmpfs /run/user/0 gets mounted on top of the previous tmpfs, clobbering the old /run/user/0, and if you then restart the quadlet while the new /run/user/0 is mounted it appears to not be able to access the old /run/user/0/netns to clean it up. This results in the old netns being orphaned and never cleaned up, and also breaks using that static ip on ipvlan for the quadlet until the lxc container is restarted.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

This is maybe a bit verbose, but it was the simplest and most reliable repro I could come up with. I don't know enough about podman networking to do anything simpler. The idea is basically create an lxc container, create an ipvlan network inside it, add a quadlet with a static ip, restart the lxc container, then ssh in and try to restart the quadlet.

I used arch to make sure I got the latest podman, but I was also able to reproduce with a Debian Trixie lxc container. I did lxd init and accepted all of the defaults.


```
#!/usr/bin/bash
set -x
N=repro-arch
sudo lxc launch images:archlinux $N
sudo lxc config set $N security.nesting true
for _ in $(seq 1 30); do sudo lxc exec $N -- getent hosts archlinux.org >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done
sudo lxc exec $N -- bash -c "
  pacman -Sy --noconfirm podman openssh
  sed -i 's/#PermitRootLogin.*/PermitRootLogin yes/;s/#PermitEmptyPasswords.*/PermitEmptyPasswords yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
  grep -q 'nullok' /etc/pam.d/system-auth || sed -i '/pam_unix.so/ s/\$/ nullok/' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
  systemctl enable sshd
  passwd -d root
  podman network create --driver ipvlan -o parent=eth0 -o mode=l2 --subnet=10.8.4.0/24 --gateway=10.8.4.1 ipvlan
  mkdir -p /etc/containers/systemd
  printf '[Container]\nImage=docker.io/library/nginx\nNetwork=ipvlan:ip=10.8.4.10\n[Install]\nWantedBy=multi-user.target\n' > /etc/containers/systemd/nginx.container"
sudo lxc restart $N
for _ in $(seq 1 30); do sudo lxc exec $N -- systemctl is-active nginx --quiet && break; sleep 1; done
IP=$(sudo lxc list $N -c 4 --format csv | cut -d ' ' -f 1)

echo "=== Baseline (quadlet started pre-login) ==="
sudo lxc exec $N -- bash -c "findmnt /run/user/0/netns; podman ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}} {{.Networks}}'"

echo "=== systemctl restart nginx via ssh (session clobbers /run/user/0/netns) ==="
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o LogLevel=ERROR root@$IP "systemctl restart nginx" || true
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o LogLevel=ERROR root@$IP "findmnt /run/user/0/netns"
sudo lxc exec $N -- bash -c "systemctl status nginx --no-pager -l; journalctl -u nginx --no-pager -n 20"

echo "Cleanup: sudo lxc delete $N --force"
```

### Describe the results you received



Sshing into the LXC container and running systemctl restart nginx.service fails with `Error: netavark: add ip addr to ipvlan: Netlink error: Address already in use (os error 98)`

Full logs:

### Describe the results you expected

I should be able to restart the quadlet after sshing in with `systemctl restart nginx.service`

### Additional information

I originally ran into this in proxmox ve 9.1, and then was able to replicate on a cachyos host and in an ubuntu 25.10 vm.

comments.body: I will take a deeper look. This will need deeper investigation.
I checked that, but I'm not sure where the problem could be. PTAL @Luap99

The problem is quite simple I think, the code tries to use XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/netns when running in a userns. In LXC even it runs as root (uid 0) it runs in a userns and thus takes the code path of XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/netns which the code will default to /run/user/0 since XDG_RUNTIME_DIR. When no user is logged in then /run/user/0 does not exists yet so our code creates and uses it.

However later if you actually login is as root users then then systemd creates a user session sets XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, and then mounts a fresh tmpfs /run/user/0 hence overmouting anything podman already created there.

At the risk of causing some regressions the netns code should not use /run/user/0 when running as uid 0, i.e. switch the code from is userns to a if uid > 0 condition.

To be clear the ssh into the lxc container is triggering the pam login stack so systemd creates a user session in this case, if you would never SSH (or otherwise trigger the login as root) it likely works fine as is.
